description:
You are an edge–computing engineer preparing a batch-update payload for a fleet of
    IoT devices.  
    A comma-separated sensor log already exists at:

        /home/user/data/edge/sensor_raw.csv

    Its current contents are exactly 5 lines (one header + four data rows) and look
    like this:

        timestamp,device_id,tempC,humidity,pressurePa
        2023-06-15T12:00:00Z,node-01,35.1,45,101325
        2023-06-15T12:01:00Z,node-01,35.3,44,101327
        2023-06-15T12:02:00Z,node-02,30.0,50,101300
        2023-06-15T12:03:00Z,node-03,40.2,38,101400

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Create a new directory called  

           /home/user/deploy

       (Only if it does not already exist.)

    2. Use standard GNU coreutils tools (specifically **cut** and **paste**—no
       `awk`, `sed`, `csvtool`, etc.) to create a final file named  

           /home/user/deploy/update_payload.csv

       that contains **only** the following three columns **in this exact order**:

        • device_id (column 2 of the source file)  
        • timestamp (column 1 of the source file)  
        • tempC     (column 3 of the source file)

       Preserve the original header names so the first line of the resulting
       file must read exactly:

           device_id,timestamp,tempC

    3. The completed /home/user/deploy/update_payload.csv must therefore contain
       precisely the following **5 lines** (note the embedded comma as the only
       delimiter and the single trailing newline at the very end of the file):

        device_id,timestamp,tempC
        node-01,2023-06-15T12:00:00Z,35.1
        node-01,2023-06-15T12:01:00Z,35.3
        node-02,2023-06-15T12:02:00Z,30.0
        node-03,2023-06-15T12:03:00Z,40.2

       The automated grader will perform an exact byte-for-byte comparison, so
       make sure:
         • there are zero extra spaces,
         • the delimiter is the literal comma character `,`,
         • there are exactly four newline characters (one after each row),
         • and no blank line appears after the final row.

    4. Intermediate helper files (e.g., /home/user/deploy/id.col or
       /home/user/deploy/ts_temp.col) are allowed and will not be graded, but the
       **final** CSV file must exist exactly as specified above.

    Constraints  
    -----------  
    • Accomplish the task using only 2–3 simple commands built around **cut** and
      **paste**.  
    • Do **not** edit sensor_raw.csv itself.  
    • You do **not** have root privileges; work entirely within /home/user.  
    • All paths are case-sensitive.

    When you are finished, you can use `cat /home/user/deploy/update_payload.csv`
    to visually confirm the result before signalling completion.
truth:
<!-- 1. File that already exists when the task starts -->
    <initial_file path="/home/user/data/edge/sensor_raw.csv"><![CDATA[
timestamp,device_id,tempC,humidity,pressurePa
2023-06-15T12:00:00Z,node-01,35.1,45,101325
2023-06-15T12:01:00Z,node-01,35.3,44,101327
2023-06-15T12:02:00Z,node-02,30.0,50,101300
2023-06-15T12:03:00Z,node-03,40.2,38,101400
]]></initial_file>

    <!-- 2. File that must exist after the agent finishes -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/deploy/update_payload.csv"><![CDATA[
device_id,timestamp,tempC
node-01,2023-06-15T12:00:00Z,35.1
node-01,2023-06-15T12:01:00Z,35.3
node-02,2023-06-15T12:02:00Z,30.0
node-03,2023-06-15T12:03:00Z,40.2
]]></expected_file>
